The login issue is occurring because the Azure AD app client secret has expired, and since this B2C application is NGC-managed, the client secre renewed in Azure and updated in the application configuration and it restore access.
We have purged the MySQL binary log files. It resolved the issue.
We have restarted the MES applications via restart pipeline, and it resolve the issue.
The alert got resolved after some time.
We have restarted the project which resolved the “MagicXPI_InactiveProjectAlert” alert.
We have restarted the MES applications via restart pipeline and the alert got resolved.
plant was restarted unexpectedly,alert got resolved
We have cleared the logs files from <XPI Installation>\Runtime\logs\java\ directory. This resolve the alert
TreeSizeFree application was consuming more memory. After closing this application alert was resolved.
We have purge the Mysql Binary logs which resolves the laert.
We have purged the MySQL binary log files and it resolved the issue.
Cleared the backlog data accumulated due to too many POST requests. This resolved the issue.
The issue was caused by a mismatch in the Activity Logs display settings file name. After correcting the file name and redeploying the project, the issue has been resolved.
'Issue was resoved after we restarted the ShopifyToSyspro project.
We have purged the MySQL Binary logs to free the disk space. It resolved the issue.
Alert resolved. We have cleared the old temp files from C:\Utils\minio\data\temp\” directory.
Alert Resolved, We have restarted the sql sever.
We have cleared the old temp files from C:\Utils\minio\data\temp\” directory and alert got resolved.
We cleared the temp files from “C:\Utils\minio\data\temp\” directory

This issue has been resolved after restarting the local agent service for now. Although we have observed that both Staging and Production are using the same Local Agent installation, as the Agent ID is identical in both web consoles which is not a recommended practice.
We have restarted the 'ngcaimldashboard' app service using application restart pipeline and it resolved the issue.
The alert was raised due to few minuts of missing metrics although the applications were up and running. Alert was auto resolved after some time.
We have cleared the old temp files from C:\Utils\minio\data\temp\” directory using cleanuplog.bat file.
We have purged the MySQL binary log files .
will need to clean up activity logs.
We have restarted the SQL server and SAP and Request projects which helped to free the memory consumption. It resolved the issue.

Customer has closed the case befor we investigate and reply them. received following update from delivery team - I could see the order number is accessible now into MDE and MES. Could you please check.
We have restarted the SQL server and SAP project which helped to free the memory consumption. It resolved the issue.

A significant memory spike in the SF_Magento_V4 project pod resulted in memory consumption exceeding 80% of the EKS node’s total capacity. This led to node instability and eventual crash, causing all associated Cloud Native services to become unavailable. Action Taken: Redeployed the impacted project in production.
The alert has been resolved. We can see the data started flowing again. Issue has been resolved now.
The alert has been resolved for CIN plant. We can see the data started flowing again. Issue has been resolved now.
Alert is auto resolved after some time though projects are running in monitor
We observed that Redis Container Instance – ‘ngcdigitaltwin’ was restarted on its own. Although, it was running and working as expected.
We have restarted the MES applications via restart pipeline, and it resolve the issue.
A client secret expired.The issue is resolved and the client secret is now good
Node memory usage increased beyond 85%, triggering the alert. To prevent a potential environment crash, we immediately redeployed the project.
opened jira and emailed to the stakeholders
We have purged the MySQL binary log files . The current free disk space is 41.0 GB.
Alert was raised due to missing metrics. After sometime alert was resolved automatically.
After sometime alert was resolved automatically.
We have restarted the ImportDocumentsIntoImageNow project as requested.
we have restarted the MES application.The issue is resolved now.
Redis was working as expected after restart so We have executed configuration commands.It resolved the alert.
We hotswap the last working build from TIL-159.After this we did not receive any alert emails for this alert.
APL-APPSVR was powered off. Team investigated the issues and resumed access to APL-APPSVR.
we can see the data is flowing for the plant. Issue is resolved.
We have restarted the MES applications using pipeline. Currently the application is up and running.
The affected EKS node was rebooted, redeployed the project.
Alert was resolved automatically.Currently all applications are running and active.
Node crash observed due to high memory usage. Team started the project after environment was stable again.
We have restarted one of the server engines.It resolved the issue.
We hotswap the last working build from TIL-161.It resolved the issue.
We have started/restarted the MES application via pipeline. Currently the application is up and running.
We corrected the server offset time for all the affected plants and it resolved the issue.
Issue got resolved after restarting the SAP project and sql server
This issue is resolved after restarting the MES application from the pipeline.
The issue has been successfully resolved after restarting the Local Agent service and restarted the projects [AgileAddressBook/ NationwideAR / JDE_emailforms / Navan]
As we were getting frequent TileBar-Kubernetes Pods Restarting alert for project pod. Restart count reached above 10. We had to redeploy the project to resolve this issue.
Power is restored to the Plant again.We can see that the data has started flowing for the CLB plant. This alert has been resolved now.
ServiceDesk checked and resolved the issue.
The problem resolved automatically once the write lock on MySQL was released, after which the rule engine service returned an active state.
Application was working. The issue got auto resolved after some time.
We have purged the MySQL binary log files which resolved the issue.
We have cleared the temporary log files which resolved the issue.
We have redeployed sf-magento-v4 project with the latest build provided by customer in deployment request TIL-177.
We have cleared the logs from the following locations: - C:\Magic xpi 4.13.5\Runtime\Logs\java - C:\Utils\minio\data\temp (Temporary files created by minio process) and alert got resolved.
We have restarted the MES application using pipeline. Currently the application is up and running.
Issue was auto resoved after some time. Data Started flowing again for JAX plant.
Issue was resoved by Service Desk
After some time, data started flowing again for WAK plant and the issue was resolved.
The plant was offline due to a storm currently in the area. Issue resolved after power restoration.
Customer have manually restarted this service and can see data flowing again.
All network and power issues have been resolved and we are reading data again.
We have redployed the project which resolves the issue
With cloud team's assistance, team managed to recover the environment, and it resolved the issue.
We executed command as per sop it resolves issue.
We have purged the MySQL binary log files.
Hotswap with latest build.(SMC-93 from Jira)
We have restarted the MES applications via restart pipeline.
Redis was working as expected after restart, so We have executed configuration commands.
We have cleared the old temp files from C:\Utils\minio\data\temp\” directory .
purge the binary logs created by MySQL local database on NGCXPAPRODVM
We have cleared the old temp files from "C:\Utils\minio\data\temp\” directory
We have restarted local agent connector service form LA web console and restarted the Europe_CEE project.

This issue happened due to the scheduled maintenance of Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instance ngcaimlanomalydatabase. This resulted in database connection loss with AIML Dashboard application. We have restarted the 'ngcaimldashboard' app service using application restart pipeline and it resolved the issue.
We observed the issue occurred due to few minutes of missing metrics. Issue was auto resolved after some time.
We have cleared the old temp files from "C:\Utils\minio\data\temp\” directory. Alert is resolved. We ran Main_New.bat from %XPI_INSTALLATION%\tool\Main_New.bat.
we cleared the older logs from "C:\inetpub\logs\LogFiles\W3SVC1\" which resolved the issue.
service desk checking ROT plant, they will update us once its started
We have cleared the old temp files.
We have restarted the SAP and State project.
Received no response from ServiceDesk. Alert auto-resolved.
We have run Cleanup_log_new from Task Scheduler.
We have restarted the Stats and SAP project.
We have cleared the logs from the following locations: - C:\Magic xpi 4.13.5\Runtime\Logs\java - C:\Utils\minio\data\temp (Temporary files created by minio process)
The iHistorian Archiver service did not re-start automatically after patching. It has been manually restarted by ServiceDesk.
There was data collection issue. ServiceDesk resolved the issue and date started flowing again.
We have restarted the MSSQL service and SAP project.
We have restarted the 'ngcdigitaltwin' Redis instance and executed the configuration commands. resolved the issue .
We have restarted the 'ngcaimldashboard' app service using application restart pipeline and it resolved the issue.
Issue was auto resoved after some time. Data Started flowing again for SAV plant.
Alert is resolved. There was power outage due to storm.
We observed the missing metrics due to which alert was raised.After some time, the alert was auto resolved.
We have observed a few errors in the AIML rule engine .Our team did few changes in code .The AIML engine is now working as expected.
We have restarted the engine for the SF_Magento_V4 project.
We restored the required config and executable files in utility, and it resolved the issue.
There is power outage at plant.
Alert was raised due to few minutes of missing metrics, and it was resolved in some time.
We have executed the configuration commands.
